sn.s: De Lukas und ich redet über Politik.
sn.e: Lukas and I talk about politics.

sn.s: "Ich glaube, d'Stadt bruucht meh Velowäg," seit er.
sn.e: "I think the city needs more bike paths," he says.

sn.s: "Ich verstah dis Argumänt, aber ich bi anderer Meinig."
sn.e: "I understand your argument, but I disagree."
"I think the problem isn't the bike paths."
"The problem is the streets, which are bad."

sn.s: "Obwohl ich find, bedi Sache sind wichtig."
Diskutiere ohni Striit isch e Kunscht.
sn.e: Arguing without fighting is an art.

sn.s: Ich han glernt, mini Meinig mit Respekt z'säge.
sn.e: I've learned to give my opinion with respect.

sn.s: Me cha anderer Meinig sii und doch Fründe blibe.
sn.e: You can disagree and still stay friends.

sn.s: E Idee verteidige uf Schwiizerdütsch git mer Sicherheit.
sn.e: Defending an idea in Swiss German gives me confidence.

sn.s: Jetz lose ich nöd nu zue: jetz red ich mit.
sn.e: Now I don't just listen: now I take part.
